items.name: Multiattack
entries: The fox makes two Tail attacks.

items.name: Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1 piercing damage, and the target must make a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw, taking 4 ({@damage 1d8}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

items.name: Beaks
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}28 ({@damage 8d6}) piercing damage, or 14 ({@damage 4d6}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer.

items.name: Running Leap
entries: With a 5-foot running start, the pumecha can long jump up to 35 feet.

items.name: Pounce
entries: If the pumecha moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the pumecha can make one bite attack against it as a bonus action.

items.name: Force Weakness
entries: When the construct takes 10 or more force damage in a single instance, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 10 or lower, it falls {@condition prone} and is {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Overcharge
entries: Whenever the construct is subjected to lightning damage, it takes no damage and instead redirects the lightning. Each non-construct creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a {@footnote VDC|14/16/17} Dexterity saving throw or take lightning damage equal to twice the original lightning damage to which the construct was subjected.

entries: When the fey takes 5 or more bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage in a single instance from a nonmagical weapon made with iron, it has {@b disadvantage} on the next ability check, saving throw, or attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

items.name: Fey Heritage
entries: Magic can't put the fey to sleep.

trait.name: Accidental Acid
entries: The black puddling is very excitable and may try to celebrate victory in combat by hugging or high-fiving a friendly creature. That creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 1d4} acid damage.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) bludgeoning damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) acid damage.

reaction.name: Adapt (3/Day)
entries: When the black puddling takes damage, it can gain resistance to the triggering damage type until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Busy Hands
entries: The handipede loves to touch new things and often feels the impulse to do so even when it is inappropriate or inconvenient.

trait.name: Sign Language
entries: The handipede can communicate simple ideas by using hand signs and gestures.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The handipede can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Chill Touch
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 4} to hit, range 120 ft., one creature. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d8}) necrotic damage, and the target can't regain hit points until the start of the handipede's next turn. If the target is undead, it has disadvantage on attack rolls against the handipede until the end of the handipede's next turn.

action.name: Sceptre
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Clingy
entries: The mecharmadillo loathes being alone, and performs poorly in the absence of company. It has disadvantage on ability checks and saving throws made against being {@condition frightened} if there are no friendly creatures that it can see or hear within 60 feet of it. It tries to remain with friendly creatures even if doing so may be dangerous or inconvenient.

action.name: Headbutt
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) bludgeoning damage.

reaction.name: Project Shield
entries: The mecharmadillo adds a bonus equal to its proficiency bonus to its AC against one melee attack that would hit it. To do so, the mecharmadillo must see the attacker.

trait.name: Hunter's Instincts
entries: The redwing is easily distracted by small prey animals. Whenever it sees a Tiny beast it hasn't seen before, it must succeed on a {@dc 11} Wisdom saving throw or get distracted. While distracted, it spends its movement and actions chasing and attacking the beast. The redwing can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns. On a success, it stops being distracted and can't get distracted by the same creature again.

trait.name: Keen Sight
entries: The redwing has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

action.name: Steel Wing
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) slashing damage.

trait.name: Accidental Arsonist
entries: The waxolotl tends to be a little careless when excited. While its candles are lit, it may occasionally and unintentionally set small flammable objects on fire.

trait.name: Hardened Wax
entries: Whenever the waxolotl takes cold damage, its speed is halved and it can't take reactions until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Heated Weapons
entries: Whenever the waxolotl takes fire damage, its attacks deal extra fire damage equal to one roll of its Hit Die until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Ember
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 10 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) fire damage.

action.name: Illumination
entries: The waxolotl can use its action to light or extinguish the candles on its body. While the candles are lit, the waxolotl sheds bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet. The candles can also be extinguished if doused, by winds of moderate speed, or if the waxolotl drops to 0 hit points.

trait.name: Fey Heritage
entries: Magic can't put the squirebug to sleep.

trait.name: Overeager
entries: When a friendly creature the squirebug can see within 30 feet of it makes an ability check, the squirebug must make a {@dc 11} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the squirebug uses its reaction to move up to its speed towards the creature. If the squirebug gets within 5 feet of the creature, it desperately tries to help and, in its eagerness, imposes disadvantage on the creature's check. Once the squirebug makes this saving throw, it doesn't do so again until the next dawn.

action.name: Horns
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Help (2/Day)
entries: The squirebug takes the Help action.

trait.name: Acidic Weapons
entries: The ooze knight's melee weapon attacks are magical. When the ooze knight hits with any weapon attack, the attack deals an extra 2 ({@damage 1d4}) acid damage on a hit (included in the attacks below).

trait.name: Corrode Metal
entries: Any nonmagical weapon made of metal that hits the ooze knight corrodes. After dealing damage, the weapon takes a permanent and cumulative -1 penalty to damage rolls. If its penalty drops to -5, the weapon is destroyed. Nonmagical ammunition made of metal that hits the ooze knight is destroyed after dealing damage.
The ooze knight can eat through 2-inch-thick, nonmagical metal in 1 round.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If the ooze knight fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The ooze knight doesn't require air or sleep.

trait.name: Opposing Reactive Mutations
entries: Whenever the ooze knight takes a certain type of damage, it gains resistance to that damage type and vulnerability to the opposing damage type. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until this trait is used again. The damage type pairs are as follows: bludgeoning and piercing; cold and fire; force and psychic; lightning and thunder; necrotic and radiant.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ooze knight makes two attacks: one with its Sword and one with its Shield Bash. It can replace one of these with its Slime Ball.

action.name: Sword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) slashing damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) acid damage.

action.name: Shield Bash
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) acid damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Slime Ball {@recharge 5}
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit +6} to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d6}) acid damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or have its speed halved for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the start of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

legendary.name: Ground Slam
entries: The ooze knight slams the ground with its shield, creating a shockwave. Each creature on the ground within 15 feet of the ooze knight must succeed on a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

legendary.name: Recharge Slime Ball
entries: The ooze knight recharges its Slime Ball attack.

legendary.name: Rush (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The ooze knight moves up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks and makes one Sword attack.

entries: The dragon exhales a spray of corrosive ooze in a 5-foot-wide, 15-foot-long line. A creature can use its action to make a {@dc 14} Strength check, ending the {@condition restrained} condition on itself or another creature within its reach on a success. action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dragon can use its Frightful Presence. It then makes two attacks: one with its Bite and one with its Tail.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) acid damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +9} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d10 + 5}) slashing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) acid damage.

action.name: Frightful Presence
entries: Each creature of the dragon's choice that is within 120 feet of the dragon and aware of it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the dragon's Frightful Presence for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Ooze Breath {@recharge 5}

Each creature in that line must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking 35 ({@damage 10d6}) acid damage and becoming {@condition restrained} in the ooze on a failed save, or half as much damage and not being {@condition restrained} on a successful one. entries: The dragon beats its wings. Each creature within 10 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw or take 9 ({@damage 1d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage and be knocked {@condition prone}. The dragon can then fly up to half its flying speed.

trait.name: Acidic Weapons
entries: The dragon's melee weapon attacks are magical. When the dragon hits with any melee weapon attack, the attack deals an extra 4 ({@damage 1d8}) acid damage (included in the attacks below).

trait.name: Corrode Metal
entries: Any nonmagical weapon made of metal that hits the dragon corrodes. After dealing damage, the weapon takes a permanent and cumulative -1 penalty to damage rolls. If its penalty drops to -5, the weapon is destroyed. Nonmagical ammunition made of metal that hits the dragon is destroyed after dealing damage.
The dragon can eat through 2-inch-thick, nonmagical metal in 1 round.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The dragon doesn't require air or sleep.

trait.name: Opposing Reactive Mutation
entries: Whenever the dragon takes a certain type of damage, it gains resistance to that damage type and vulnerability to the opposing damage type. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until this trait is used again. The damage type pairs are as follows: bludgeoning and piercing; cold and fire; force and psychic; lightning and thunder; necrotic and radiant.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dragon can use its Frightful Presence. It then makes two attacks: one with its Bite and one with its Tail.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +13} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 2d10 + 8}) piercing damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) acid damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +13} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 1d12 + 8}) bludgeoning damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) acid damage.

action.name: Frightful Presence
entries: Each creature of the dragon's choice that is within 120 feet of the dragon and aware of it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the dragon's Frightful Presence for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Ooze Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The dragon exhales a spray of corrosive ooze in a 5-foot-wide, 90-foot line. Each creature in that line must make a {@dc 20} Dexterity saving throw, taking 42 ({@damage 12d6}) acid damage and becoming {@condition restrained} in the ooze on a failed save, or half as much damage and not being {@condition restrained} on a successful one. A creature can use its action to make a {@dc 20} Strength check, ending the {@condition restrained} condition on itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Nonmagical metal in the area corrodes as if it hit the dragon as described in the Corrode Metal trait.

legendary.name: Tail
entries: The dragon makes a Tail attack.

legendary.name: Psychic Blast (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The dragon implants false memories into the minds of three creatures it can see within 60 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Intelligence saving throw or immediately use its reaction to make one attack against a randomly-determined target within reach/range other than the dragon.

legendary.name: Wing Attack (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The dragon beats its wings. Each creature within 10 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 21} Dexterity saving throw or take 15 ({@damage 2d6 + 8}) bludgeoning damage and be knocked {@condition prone}. The dragon can then fly up to half its flying speed.

monster.name: Emaciated Bloodhound

trait.name: Aversion of Fire
entries: If the Bloodhound takes fire damage, he has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of his next turn.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The Bloodhound is immune to any spell or effect that would alter his form.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The Bloodhound has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (1/Day)
entries: If the Bloodhound fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The Bloodhound has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the Bloodhound's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Unleash the Beast (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: If the Bloodhound would be reduced to 0 hit points, his current hit point total instead resets to 52 hit points, he regains any expended use of Legendary Resistance, and he gains a flying speed of 40 feet for the next hour. Additionally, the Bloodhound can now use the options in the "Mythic Actions" section for 1 hour. Award a party an additional 700 XP (1,150 XP total) for defeating the Bloodhound after his Unleash the Beast activates.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Bloodhound makes two attacks: one with his Bite and one with his Claws. He can replace any one attack with an attack from his Nailbow.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d10 + 1}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 2d4 + 1}) slashing damage.

action.name: Nailbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit +3} to hit, range 100/400 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d8 + 1}) piercing damage.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The Bloodhound makes one attack with his Claws or Nailbow.

legendary.name: Shadow Jump
entries: If the Bloodhound is in dim light or darkness, he teleports to another area of dim light or darkness within 30 feet of him that he can see and takes the Hide action.

mythicHeader: If the Bloodhound's Unleash the Beast trait has activated in the last hour, he can use the options below as legendary actions.

mythic.name: Shadow Pounce
entries: The Bloodhound uses his Shadow Jump legendary action option and then makes one attack with his Bite or Claws.

mythic.name: Razor Gust (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Bloodhound beats his wings in a cloud of metal shards. Each creature in a 30-foot cone must make a {@dc 11} Dexterity saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) slashing damage on a failure or half as much damage on a success. The Bloodhound can then fly up to half his flying speed.

trait.name: Aversion of Fire
entries: If the Bloodhound takes fire damage, he has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of his next turn.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The Bloodhound is immune to any spell or effect that would alter his form.

entries: If the Bloodhound would be reduced to 0 hit points, his current hit point total instead resets to 110 hit points, he gains 50 temporary hit points, he regains any expended uses of Legendary Resistance, and he gains a flying speed of 60 feet for the next hour. Additionally, the Bloodhound can now use the options in the "Mythic Actions" section for 1 hour. Award a party an additional 3,900 XP (6,200 XP total) for defeating the Bloodhound after his Unleash the Beast activates.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Bloodhound makes two attacks: one with his Bite and one with his Claws. He can replace any one attack with an attack from his Nailbow.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d12 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) slashing damage.

action.name: Nailbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit +6} to hit, range 100/400 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d10 + 3}) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Sanguine Mark (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: The Bloodhound causes ethereal, glowing, barbed wire to wrap around one creature he can see within 90 feet of him for the next 10 minutes. For the duration, whenever the Bloodhound deals damage to that creature for the first time on a turn, the creature takes an additional 3 ({@damage 1d6}) necrotic damage and the Bloodhound regains an equal number of hit points. If the target drops to 0 hit points before this effect ends, the Bloodhound can use a bonus action on a subsequent turn to mark a new creature.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The Bloodhound makes one attack with his Claws or Nailbow.

legendary.name: Shadow Jump
entries: If the Bloodhound is in dim light or darkness, he teleports to another area of dim light or darkness within 40 feet of him that he can see and takes the Hide action.

mythicHeader: If the Bloodhound's Unleash the Beast trait has activated in the last hour, he can use the options below as legendary actions.

mythic.name: Shadow Pounce
entries: The Bloodhound uses his Shadow Jump legendary action option and then makes one attack with his Bite or Claws.

mythic.name: Razor Gust (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Bloodhound beats his wings in a cloud of metal shards. Each creature in a 40-foot cone must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) slashing damage on a failure or half as much damage on a success. The Bloodhound can then fly up to half his flying speed.

trait.name: Unleash the Beast (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: If the Bloodhound would be reduced to 0 hit points, his current hit point total instead resets to 152 hit points, he gains 50 temporary hit points, he regains any expended uses of Legendary Resistance, and he gains a flying speed of 60 feet for the next hour. Additionally, the Bloodhound can now use the options in the "Mythic Actions" section for 1 hour. Award a party an additional 11,500 XP (18,700 XP total) for defeating the Bloodhound after his Unleash the Beast activates.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Bloodhound makes three attacks: one with his Bite and two with his Claws. He can replace up to two attacks with attacks from his Nailbow.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d12 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Nailbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit +8} to hit, range 100/400 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d10 + 4}) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Sanguine Mark (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: The Bloodhound causes ethereal, glowing, barbed wire to wrap around one creature he can see within 90 feet of him for the next 10 minutes. For the duration, whenever the Bloodhound deals damage to that creature with an attack, the creature takes an additional 3 ({@damage 1d6}) necrotic damage and the Bloodhound regains an equal number of hit points. If the target drops to 0 hit points before this effect ends, the Bloodhound can use a bonus action on a subsequent turn to mark a new creature.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The Bloodhound makes one attack with his Claws or Nailbow.

legendary.name: Shadow Jump
entries: If the Bloodhound is in dim light or darkness, he teleports to another area of dim light or darkness within 60 feet of him that he can see and takes the Hide action.

mythicHeader: If the Bloodhound's Unleash the Beast trait has activated in the last hour, it can use the options below as legendary actions.

mythic.name: Shadow Pounce
entries: The Bloodhound uses his Shadow Jump legendary action option and then makes one attack with his Bite or Claws.

mythic.name: Razor Gust (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Bloodhound beats his wings in a cloud of metal shards. Each creature in a 60-foot cone must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking 28 ({@damage 8d6}) slashing damage on a failure or half as much damage on a success. The Bloodhound can then fly up to half his flying speed.

immune.note: from nonmagical attacks that aren't adamantine

trait.name: Constructed Nature
entries: CREO doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Electrified
entries: CREO deals an extra 2 ({@damage 1d4}) lightning damage when it hits with a weapon attack (included in attacks).

trait.name: Force Weakness
entries: When CREO takes 10 or more force damage in a single instance, it must succeed on a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw or fall {@condition prone} and regain one fewer legendary actions at the start of its next turn (minimum 0).

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If CREO fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Overcharge
entries: Whenever CREO is subjected to lightning damage, it takes no damage and instead redirects the lightning. Each non-construct creature within 10 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw or take lightning damage equal to twice the original lightning damage to which CREO was subjected.

trait.name: Shapechanger
entries: CREO can use its action to {@spell polymorph} into a Small or Medium humanoid, or back into its true form. Other than its size and speed, its statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: CREO makes two attacks with any combination of Claw and Nailbow.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) slashing damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) lightning damage.

action.name: Nailbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit +7} to hit, range 100/400 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d10 + 4}) piercing damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) lightning damage.

action.name: Wax Fountain {@recharge 5}
entries: CREO splashes hot wax in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) fire damage and becoming {@condition restrained} on a failure, or half as much damage and not becoming {@condition restrained} on a success. A creature can take an action to attempt a {@dc 14} Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach and ending the {@condition restrained} condition on a success.

legendary.name: Analyse & Recalibrate
entries: CREO targets a creature it can see within 60 feet of it and analyses it. Until the end of CREO's next turn, it has advantage on attack rolls it makes against that target. In addition, when it takes this action, CREO can change the bonus damage type of its Electrified trait to any of the following: acid, cold, fire, lightning, necrotic, poison, psychic, radiant, or thunder.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: CREO makes one Claw or Nailbow attack.

legendary.name: Slime Ball (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: CREO launches a huge ball of slime at a point within 120 feet of it, where it explodes in a 20-foot radius. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw, taking 7 ({@damage 3d4}) acid damage and having its speed halved for 1 minute on a failure, or half as much damage and not affecting its speed on a success. A creature can take an action to attempt a {@dc 14} Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach and ending the {@condition restrained} condition on a success.

entries: Unit M3T4L exhales a blast of tiny machines in a 90-foot cone that start tearing down all matter in the affected area. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 20} Constitution saving throw, taking 56 ({@damage 16d6}) force damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Any nonmagical weapon or armour in the affected area corrodes. After dealing damage, a corroded weapon takes a permanent and cumulative −1 penalty to damage rolls. If its penalty drops to −5, the weapon is destroyed. Corroded armour takes a permanent and cumulative −1 penalty to the AC it offers. Armour reduced to an AC of 10 or a shield that drops to a +0 bonus is destroyed.

legendary.name: Analyse
entries: Unit M3T4L targets a creature it can see within 60 feet of it and analyses its motion. Until the end of Unit M3T4L's next turn, it has advantage on attack rolls it makes against that target.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: Unit M3T4L makes one Taser Claw attack.

legendary.name: Holographic Mirage
entries: Unit M3T4L becomes invisible at the same time it projects a holographic illusory duplicate that appears where it was standing. It then immediately makes a Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) check to hide and moves up to half its speed. If an attack hits the duplicate or Unit M3T4L takes any action, the duplicate is destroyed and Unit M3T4L is no longer invisible.

immune.note: from nonmagical attacks that aren't adamantine

trait.name: Constructed Nature
entries: Unit M3T4L doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Force Weakness
entries: When Unit M3T4L takes 20 or more force damage in a single instance, it must succeed on a {@dc 27} Constitution saving throw or fall {@condition prone} and regain one fewer legendary actions at the start of its next turn (minimum 0).

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: Unit M3T4L is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Unit M3T4L fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: Unit M3T4L has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: Unit M3T4L's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Overcharge
entries: Whenever Unit M3T4L is subjected to lightning damage, it takes no damage and instead redirects the lightning. Each non-construct creature within 10 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw or take lightning damage equal to twice the original lightning damage to which Unit M3T4L was subjected.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Unit M3T4L can use its Pneumatic Spray. It then makes three attacks: one with its Arc Blade and two with its Taser Claw.

action.name: Arc Blade
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit +18} to hit, range 60/240 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 2d10 + 10}) slashing damage and 6 ({@damage 1d12}) lightning damage.

action.name: Taser Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +18} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d6 + 10}) slashing damage and 6 ({@damage 1d12}) lightning damage. The target must also succeed on a {@dc 24} Constitution saving throw or be unable to take reactions until the start of its next turn.

action.name: Pneumatic Spray
entries: Unit M3T4L sprays conductive fluid from a nozzle in its maw in a 20-foot cone. Each creature in the area must succeed on a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw or become drenched in the fluid and gain vulnerability to lightning damage until the start of Unit M3T4L's next turn.

action.name: Nanobot Blast {@recharge 5}
entries: Unit M3T4L exhales a blast of tiny machines in a 90-foot cone that start tearing down all matter in the affected area. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 24} Constitution saving throw, taking 71 ({@damage 13d10}) force damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Any nonmagical weapon or armour in the affected area corrodes. After dealing damage, a corroded weapon takes a permanent and cumulative −1 penalty to damage rolls. If its penalty drops to −5, the weapon is destroyed. Corroded armour takes a permanent and cumulative −1 penalty to the AC it offers. Armour reduced to an AC of 10 or a shield that drops to a +0 bonus is destroyed.

legendary.name: Analyse
entries: Unit M3T4L targets a creature it can see within 60 feet of it and analyses its motion. Until the end of Unit M3T4L's next turn, it has advantage on attack rolls it makes against that target.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: Unit M3T4L makes one Taser Claw attack.

legendary.name: Holographic Mirage
entries: Unit M3T4L becomes invisible at the same time it projects a holographic illusory duplicate that appears where it was standing. It then immediately makes a Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) check to hide and moves up to half its speed. If an attack hits the duplicate or Unit M3T4L takes any action, the duplicate is destroyed and Unit M3T4L is no longer invisible.

monster.name: Hexahedrooze

trait.name: Reactive Mutations
entries: Immediately after the ooze takes damage, it gains resistance to that damage type. The ooze can have up to two resistances from this trait. If the ooze has its maximum number of resistances, the oldest resistance is lost when a new one is gained.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The ooze can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ooze makes two Pseudopod attacks. It can replace one attack with Engulf.

action.name: Engulf
entries: The ooze moves up to its speed. While doing so, it can enter Small or smaller  creatures' spaces. Whenever the ooze enters a creature's space, the creature must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw.
On a successful save, the creature can choose to be pushed 5 feet back or to the side of the ooze. A creature that chooses not to be pushed suffers the consequences of a failed saving throw.

On a failed save, the ooze enters the creature's space, and the creature takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) acid damage and is engulfed. The engulfed creature can't breathe, is {@condition restrained}, and takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) acid damage at the start of each of the ooze's turns. When the ooze moves, the engulfed creature moves with it.
An engulfed creature can try to escape by taking an action to make a {@dc 13} Strength check. On a success, the creature escapes and enters a space of its choice within 5 feet of the ooze.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d6}) acid damage and the target suffers one of the following random effects (roll a {@dice d6}):
1. The target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) force damage.
2. The target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) bludgeoning damage.
3. The target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) piercing damage.
4. The target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) slashing damage.
5. The target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) cold damage.
6. The target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage.

legendaryHeader: The ooze can take 1 legendary action, choosing the option below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The ooze regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The ooze makes one Pseudopod attack

2. The target takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) bludgeoning damage.
3. The target takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) piercing damage.
4. The target takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) slashing damage.
5. The target takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) cold damage.
6. The target takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) fire damage.
7. The target takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) lightning damage.
8. The target takes 4 ({@damage 1d8})thunder damage.

entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d8}) acid damage and the target suffers one of the following random effects (roll a {@dice d10}):
1. The target takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) force damage.
2. The target takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) bludgeoning damage.
3. The target takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) piercing damage.
4. The target takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) slashing damage.
5. The target takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) cold damage.
6. The target takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) fire damage.
7. The target takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) lightning damage.
8. The target takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) thunder damage.
9. The target takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) necrotic damage.
10. The target takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) radiant damage

legendaryHeader: The ooze can take 2 legendary action, choosing the option below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The ooze regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The ooze makes one Pseudopod attack

monster.name: Celestial Devourer Parvus

trait.name: Angelic Weapons
entries: The devourer's weapon attacks are magical. When the devourer hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 7 ({@damage 2d6}) radiant damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Feast of Kings (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: If the devourer would be reduced to 0 hit points, his current hit point total instead resets to 90 hit points, his Decaying Blast recharges, he regains any expended use of Legendary Resistance, and he can now use the options in the "Mythic Actions" section for 1 hour. In addition, the devourer's body morphs as a flurry of appendages erupts from his back to form grotesque wings. The devourer gains a flying speed of 120 feet. Award a party 2,900 XP (5,800 XP total) for defeating the devourer after he uses Feast of Kings.

trait.name: Healing Weakness
entries: Whenever a spell or other magical effect would restore hit points to the devourer, he doesn't regain hit points and instead takes necrotic damage equal to the number of hit points that would have been restored.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (1/Day)
entries: If the devourer fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devourer makes two Greatsword attacks and a Wing Strike attack if his Feast of Kings trait has been activated within the last hour.

action.name: Greatsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) slashing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) radiant damage.

action.name: Divine Grasp
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit +7} to hit, range 120 ft., one creature. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) radiant damage, and the target can't regain hit points until the start of the devourer's next turn.

action.name: Decaying Blast {@recharge 5}
entries: The devourer unleashes a blast of necrotic energy. Each creature within 30 feet of the devourer must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

legendary.name: Divine Sight
entries: The devourer's eyes flash with golden light, and he gains {@sense truesight} out to 60 feet until the end of his next turn.

legendary.name: Celestial Feast (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The devourer pulls a piece of blood-covered, sparkling meat from a hidden pocket, consumes it, and regains 10 ({@damage 3d6}) hit points. The devourer then chooses one creature that he can see. The targeted creature is instantly bathed in a scintillating light and is freed from any curse, disease, poison, blindness, or deafness.

legendary.name: Royal Reach (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The devourer uses Divine Grasp.

legendary.name: Teleport (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The devourer magically teleports in a flash of light, along with any equipment he is wearing or carrying, up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space he can see. Each creature within 10 feet of the devourer before he teleported must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the start of its next turn.

mythicHeader: If the devourer's Feast of Kings trait has activated in the last hour, he can use the options below as legendary actions.

mythic.name: Convert (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The devourer targets one humanoid he can see within 30 feet of him and ropes of golden light shoot out of his wings' hands. If the target can see the devourer, the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by the devourer for the next 24 hours. A creature {@condition charmed} in this way is under the complete control of the devourer. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

Each time the devourer or his allies do anything harmful to the target, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success. The effect ends early if the devourer is destroyed, is on a different plane of existence than the target, takes a bonus action to end the effect, or if another creature fails a saving throw against this action.

mythic.name: Wing Strike (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The devourer beats his wings. Each Medium or smaller creature within 10 feet of the devourer must succeed on a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw or take 7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) bludgeoning damage. The devourer can choose one creature that fails this saving throw to become {@condition grappled} by it (escape {@dc 14}).

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The pitcrow doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Mimicry
entries: The pitcrow can mimic simple sounds it has heard, such as a person whispering, a baby crying, or an animal chittering. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a successful {@dc 12} Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The pitcrow has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the pitcrow's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Vicious Mockery
entries: The pitcrow targets one creature that can hear it within 60 feet. The target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Wisdom saving throw or take 5 ({@damage 2d4}) psychic damage and have disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The murder doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Multitudinous Mimicry
entries: The murder can simultaneously mimic one or more simple sounds it has heard, such as a person whispering, a crowd chattering, animals chittering, horses galloping, or an orchestra playing. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a successful {@dc 13} Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The murder has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the murder's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The murder can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny pitcrow. The murder can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The murder makes two Swarm of Beaks attacks. Alternatively, it uses Vicious Mockery six times, or three times if the swarm has half its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Swarm of Beaks
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}27 ({@damage 6d8}) piercing damage, or 13 ({@damage 3d8}) piercing damage if the murder has half its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Vicious Mockery
entries: The murder targets one creature that can hear it within 60 feet. The target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or take 7 ({@damage 3d4}) psychic damage and have disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

monster.name: Pontiff Poco

trait.name: Heated Body
entries: A creature that touches the Pontiff or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) fire damage. If the Pontiff takes fire damage, this trait deals 5 ({@damage 2d4}) fire damage until the end of its next turn instead.

trait.name: Heated Weapons
entries: When the Pontiff hits with a metal melee weapon, it deals an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage (included in the attack). If the Pontiff takes fire damage, its attacks deal 7 ({@damage 2d6}) extra fire damage until the end of its next turn instead.

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The Pontiff sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (1/Day)
entries: If the Pontiff fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The Pontiff regains 10 hit points at the start of its turn. If the Pontiff takes cold or thunder damage, this trait doesn't function at the start of the Pontiff's next turn. The Pontiff dies only if it starts its turn with 0 hit points and doesn't regenerate.

trait.name: Shapechanger
entries: The Pontiff can use its action to {@spell polymorph} into a humanoid that it has seen, or back into its true form. Its statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

trait.name: Stained Glass Armour (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: If the Pontiff would be reduced to 0 hit points, its current hit point total instead resets to 75 hit points, its Thurible recharges, it regains any expended use of Legendary Resistance, and, for the next hour, it can use its Mythic Actions alongside its Legendary Actions. In addition, the Pontiff emits a blast of energy that shatters the stained glass windows of its lair and draws the shards into its wax body to form a protective armour. Award a party 1,100 XP (1,800 XP total) for defeating the Pontiff after it uses Stained Glass Armour.

action.name: Burning Crosier
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) bludgeoning damage, or 8 ({@damage 1d10 + 3}) bludgeoning damage if used with two hands to make a melee attack, plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Thurible {@recharge 6}
entries: The Pontiff lights the incense in its thurible and a 15-foot-radius sphere of poisonous orange smoke pours out, centred on the Pontiff. The smoke lightly obscures its area, spreads around corners, and moves with the Pontiff. It lasts for 1 minute or until strong wind disperses it, ending the effect.

When a creature enters the area for the first time on its turn or starts its turn there, it must make a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw, taking 5 ({@damage 2d4}) poison damage and becoming {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute on a failed save, or half as much damage and not becoming {@condition poisoned} on a successful one. A creature {@condition poisoned} by the smoke takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) poison damage at the start of each of its turns, and can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. A creature can avoid the effects of the smoke if it holds its breath while in the area or doesn't need to breathe.

legendary.name: Pack Thurible
entries: The Pontiff rolls to recharge its Thurible.

legendary.name: Tactical Retreat
entries: The Pontiff takes the Disengage action and moves up to half its speed. This movement cannot be toward an enemy.

legendary.name: Wax Splash (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Pontiff splashes hot wax at a creature within 10 feet of it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw or become {@condition restrained} in the hardened wax. A creature can take an action to attempt a {@dc 12} Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach and ending the {@condition restrained} condition on a success.

mythicHeader: If the Pontiff's Stained Glass Armour trait has activated in the last hour, it can use the option below as a legendary action.

mythic.name: Shard Spray (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Pontiff releases a blast of glass shards in a 20-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw, taking 9 ({@damage 2d8}) slashing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

monster.name: Pontiff Preceptor

trait.name: Heated Body
entries: A creature that touches the Pontiff or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) fire damage. If the Pontiff takes fire damage, this trait deals 9 ({@damage 2d8}) fire damage until the end of its next turn instead.

trait.name: Heated Weapons
entries: When the Pontiff hits with a metal melee weapon, it deals an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage (included in the attack). If the Pontiff takes fire damage, its attacks deal 10 ({@damage 3d6}) extra fire damage until the end of its next turn instead.

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The Pontiff sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (1/Day)
entries: If the Pontiff fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The Pontiff has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The Pontiff regains 10 hit points at the start of its turn. If the Pontiff takes cold or thunder damage, this trait doesn't function at the start of the Pontiff's next turn. The Pontiff dies only if it starts its turn with 0 hit points and doesn't regenerate.

In addition, the Pontiff emits a blast of energy that shatters the stained glass windows of its lair and draws the shards into its wax body to form a protective armour. When the Pontiff takes spell damage, the effect of a 3rd-level {@spell color spray} spell instantly bursts from the Pontiff in a 20-foot-radius sphere centred on the Pontiff. Award a party 2,900 XP (5,800 XP total) for defeating the Pontiff after it uses Stained Glass Armour.

entries: The Pontiff makes two Burning Crosier attacks.

entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage, or 10 ({@damage 1d10 + 5}) bludgeoning damage if used with two hands to make a melee attack, plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage.

mythic.name: Shard Spray (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Pontiff releases a blast of glass shards in a 40-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw, taking 18 ({@damage 4d8}) slashing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The wolf has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The wolf has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the wolf's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Sanguine Predator
entries: The wolf can magically see any creature that isn't an undead or a construct within 60 feet of it, even through total cover, heavily obscured areas, invisibility, or any other phenomena that would prevent sight. Whenever the wolf deals damage to a creature afflicted by a Sanguine Mark (see Bloodhound), the creature takes an additional 3 ({@damage 1d6}) necrotic damage and the wolf regains an equal number of hit points.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wolf makes three attacks: one with its Bite and two with its Claws.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 3d10 + 5}) piercing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) poison damage and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. While {@condition poisoned} in this way, its melee attacks deal half as much damage as normal and its speed is halved. A target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}20 ({@damage 6d4 + 5}) slashing damage.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The wolf has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The wolf has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the wolf's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Toxic Empowerment
entries: When the wolf is subjected to an effect that would cause it to be poisoned, it's empowered by the toxins instead, gaining 14 ({@damage 4d6}) temporary hit points

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Poison Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The wolf exhales a cloud of poisonous gas in a 20-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition poisoned}.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The ranger is a 6th-level spellcaster and can cast the following spells, using Wisdom as its spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 14}, {@hit 6} to hit with spell attacks):

trait.name: Land's Stride
entries: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs the ranger no extra movement. It can also pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ranger makes three attacks with its Shortsword or two attacks with its Longbow.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 9} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Volley
entries: The ranger makes a separate Longbow attack against each creature of its choice within 10 feet of a point it can see within the weapon's range.

action.name: Whirlwind
entries: The ranger makes a separate Shortsword attack against each creature of its choice within its reach.

bonus.name: Hunter's Mark (1/Short or Long Rest)
entries: The ranger chooses one creature it can see within 90 feet and mystically marks it as its quarry for 1 hour. For the duration, the ranger deals an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) damage to the target whenever it hits it with a weapon attack, and the ranger has advantage on any Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) or Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) check it makes to find the creature. If the target drops to 0 hit points before this effect ends, the ranger can use a bonus action on a subsequent turn to mark a new creature.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The sorcerer is a 7th-level spellcaster and can cast the following spells, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}, {@hit 5} to hit with spell attacks):

trait.name: Metamagic
entries: The sorcerer has 7 sorcery points and regains all expended sorcery points when it finishes a long rest. It can spend these points in the following ways:

items: {@b Quickened Spell.} When the sorcerer casts a spell that has a casting time of 1 action, it can spend 2 sorcery points to change the casting time to 1 bonus action for this casting.

{@b Twinned Spell.} When the sorcerer casts a spell that targets only one creature and doesn't have a range of self, it can spend a number of sorcery points equal to the spell's level to target a second creature in range with the same spell (1 sorcery point if the spell is a cantrip).

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit +3} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) piercing damage.

action.name: Magnetobolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit +5} to hit, range 90 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d6}) force damage and the creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. A creature made of ferrous metal or wearing ferrous armour has disadvantage on this saving throw.

action.name: Shocking Grasp
entries: {@atk ms} {@hit +5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d8}) lightning damage and the creature can't take reactions until the start of its next turn. The sorcerer has advantage on the attack roll if the target is wearing armour made of metal.

trait.name: Evasion
entries: If Adelifae is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, Adelifae instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails.

trait.name: Ferrous Weakness
entries: When Adelifae takes 4 or more bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage in a single instance from a nonmagical weapon made with iron, it has disadvantage on the next ability check, saving throw, or attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Fey Heritage
entries: Magic can't put Adelifae to sleep.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: Adelifae doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (1/Day)
entries: If Adelifae fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magical Repulsion
entries: Adelifae has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from magical weapons (included in Damage Resistances).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Adelifae makes one Proboscis attack and uses Wing Strike.

action.name: Proboscis
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +4} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage, and the target must make a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw. On a failed saving throw, a creature takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) poison damage and is {@condition charmed} by Adelifae. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition charmed}. A {@condition charmed} creature regards Adelifae as a trusted friend to be heeded and protected. Although the target isn't under Adelifae's control, it takes Adelifae's requests or actions in the most favourable way it can.

Each time Adelifae or its allies do anything harmful to the target, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success. Otherwise, the effect lasts 24 hours or until Adelifae dies, is on a different plane of existence from the target, or ends the effect as a bonus action. If a target's saving throw is successful, the target is immune to this effect for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Wing Strike
entries: Adelifae beats its wings and emits a sonic blast in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw, taking 3 ({@damage 1d6}) thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary.name: Proboscis Attack
entries: Adelifae makes a Proboscis attack.

legendary.name: Dazzling Melody (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Adelifae chooses a creature it can see within 120 feet of it and croons a song accompanied by tinkling music from its wings. If the target can hear Adelifae, it must succeed on a {@dc 12} Charisma saving throw or be {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn. The effect ends early if it takes damage or Adelifae takes another action.

legendary.name: Fey Shroud (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Adelifae becomes {@condition invisible} until the start of its next turn or it takes another action.

entries: When the Scarab takes 4 or more bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage in a single instance from a nonmagical weapon made with iron, it has disadvantage on the next ability check, saving throw, or attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Fey Heritage
entries: Magic can't put the Scarab to sleep.

trait.name: Freedom of Movement
entries: The Scarab ignores difficult terrain, and magical effects can't reduce its speed or cause it to be {@condition restrained}. It can spend 5 feet of movement to escape from nonmagical restraints or being {@condition grappled}.

trait.name: Limited Flight
entries: The Scarab can move by using its wings in bursts of flight. The Scarab is too heavy for continued flight or hovering and falls at the end of its turn if nothing but its flying speed is keeping it aloft.

trait.name: Magical Repulsion
entries: The Scarab has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slsahing damage from magical weapons (included in Damage Resistances).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Scarab makes two attacks with its Herculean Horn.

action.name: Herculean Horn
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +5} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it must make a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target is launched up to 15 feet away in a direction of the Scarab's choosing, landing {@condition prone}.

action.name: Charging Impalement {@recharge 4}
entries: The Scarab moves up to its speed in a straight line, moving through the spaces of creatures smaller than it. Each creature whose space it moves through must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take damage as if it had been hit by the Scarab's Herculean Horn attack and failed the Strength saving throw.

legendary.name: Horn Attack
entries: The Scarab makes a Herculean Horn attack.

legendary.name: Shell Defense
entries: The Scarab raises its carapace to shield itself from harm, gaining a +4 bonus to AC until it makes an attack or the start of its next turn, whichever comes first.

legendary.name: Temporal Mishap (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Scarab creates temporal fluctuations in a 10-foot-radius sphere centred on itself. Each other creature in the area must make a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw. On a failed saving throw, a creature takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) force damage and is teleported to an unoccupied space within 30 feet of the Scarab that it can see. On a successful saving throw, a creature takes half as much damage and is pushed 10 feet away from the Scarab.

trait.name: Ferrous Weakness
entries: When the Scarab takes 8 or more bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage in a single instance from a nonmagical weapon made with iron, it has disadvantage on the next ability check, saving throw, or attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Fey Heritage
entries: Magic can't put the Scarab to sleep.

trait.name: Freedom of Movement
entries: The Scarab ignores difficult terrain, and magical effects can't reduce its speed or cause it to be {@condition restrained}. It can spend 5 feet of movement to escape from nonmagical restraints or being {@condition grappled}.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If the Scarab fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Limited Flight
entries: The Scarab can move by using its wings in bursts of flight. The Scarab is too heavy for continued flight or hovering and falls at the end of its turn if nothing but its flying speed is keeping it aloft.

trait.name: Magical Repulsion
entries: The Scarab has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slahing damage from magical weapons (included in Damage Resistances).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Scarab makes two attacks with its Herculean Horn.

action.name: Herculean Horn
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it must make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target is launched up to 15 feet away in a direction of the Scarab's choosing, landing {@condition prone}.

action.name: Charging Impalement {@recharge 4}
entries: The Scarab moves up to its speed in a straight line, moving through the spaces of creatures smaller than it. Each creature whose space it moves through must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take damage as if it had been hit by the Scarab's Herculean Horn attack and failed the Strength saving throw.

entries: The Scarab makes a Herculean Horn attack.

legendary.name: Shell Defense
entries: The Scarab raises its carapace to shield itself from harm, gaining a +4 bonus to AC until it makes an attack or the start of its next turn, whichever comes first.

legendary.name: Temporal Mishap (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Scarab creates temporal fluctuations in a 10-foot-radius sphere centred on itself. Each other creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw. On a failed saving throw, a creature takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) force damage and is teleported to an unoccupied space within 30 feet of the Scarab that it can see. On a successful saving throw, a creature takes half as much damage and is pushed 10 feet away from the Scarab.

trait.name: Ferrous Weakness
entries: When the Scarab takes 12 or more bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage in a single instance from a nonmagical weapon made with iron, it has disadvantage on the next ability check, saving throw, or attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Fey Heritage
entries: Magic can't put the Scarab to sleep.

trait.name: Freedom of Movement

trait.name: Limited Flight

trait.name: Magical Repulsion
entries: The Scarab has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slahing damage from magical weapons (included in Damage Resistances).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Scarab makes two attacks with its Herculean Horn.

action.name: Herculean Horn
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d12 + 5}) piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it must make a {@dc 17} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target is launched up to 15 feet away in a direction of the Scarab's choosing, taking 2 ({@damage 1d4}) bludgeoning damage and landing {@condition prone}.

action.name: Charging Impalement {@recharge 4}
entries: The Scarab moves up to its speed in a straight line, moving through the spaces of creatures smaller than it. Each creature whose space it moves through must succeed on a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw or take damage as if it had been hit by the Scarab's Herculean Horn attack and failed the Strength saving throw.

legendary.name: Horn Attack
entries: The Scarab makes a Herculean Horn attack.

legendary.name: Shell Defense
entries: The Scarab raises its carapace to shield itself from harm, gaining a +4 bonus to AC until it makes an attack or the start of its next turn, whichever comes first.

legendary.name: Temporal Mishap (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Scarab creates temporal fluctuations in a 10-foot-radius sphere centred on itself. Each other creature in the area must make a {@dc 17} Charisma saving throw. On a failed saving throw, a creature takes 11 ({@damage 2d10}) force damage and is teleported to an unoccupied space within 30 feet of the Scarab that it can see. On a successful saving throw, a creature takes half as much damage and is pushed 10 feet away from the Scarab.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny butterfly. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Thundering Music
entries: Each creature that starts its turn in the swarm's space must succeed on a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw or take 3 ({@damage 1d6}) thunder damage.

action.name: Bites
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +4} to hit, reach 0 ft., one creature in the swarm's space. {@h}5 ({@damage 2d4}) piercing damage, or 2 ({@damage 1d4}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer.

trait.name: Bright
entries: When the sunsprite isn't invisible, it sheds bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet.

trait.name: Death Burst
entries: When the sunsprite dies, it explodes in a burst of light. Each creature within 5 feet of the sunsprite must make a {@dc 10} Dexterity saving throw, taking 7 ({@damage 2d6}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Evasion
entries: When the sunsprite is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1 piercing damage.

action.name: Sunbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit +5} to hit, range 40/160 ft., one target. {@h}1 piercing damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) radiant damage.

action.name: Invisibility
entries: The sunsprite magically turns invisible until it attacks, or until its {@status concentration} ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Any equipment the sunsprite wears or carries is invisible with it.

items.name: Immutable Form
entries: The elemental is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

items.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The elemental has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

items.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The elemental's weapon attacks are magical.

items.name: Heated Weapons
entries: Whenever the elemental is subjected to fire damage, its weapon attacks deal {@damage 1d6;3d6;4d6|Vdam} extra fire damage on a hit until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Hardened Wax
entries: Whenever the elemental is subjected to cold damage, until the end of its next turn: its speed is halved, it has vulnerability to thunder damage, and it can't take reactions until the start of its next turn.

items.name: Avoidance
entries: If the construct is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw and only half damage if it fails.

items.name: Force Weakness
entries: When the construct takes 10 or more force damage in a single instance, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 10 or lower, it falls {@condition prone} and is {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Overcharge
entries: Whenever the construct is subjected to lightning damage, it takes no damage and instead redirects the lightning. Each non-construct creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a {@footnote VDC|14/16/17} Dexterity saving throw or take lightning damage equal to twice the original lightning damage to which the construct was subjected.

Each non-construct creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a {@footnote VDC|14/16/17} Dexterity saving throw or take lightning damage equal to twice the original lightning damage to which the construct was subjected.

monsterFluff.name: Silver-Tailed Cheshire Fox
entries: These graceful foxes have ebony fur and overly long twin tails ending in razor- sharp silvery tips. The fox can control each tail independently, slicing into its prey before detaching its tri-hinged jaws and ripping chunks out of them. These territorial vulpines hunt in packs, using their natural stealthiness to ambush intruders and avoid larger predators and hunters. Some tailors pay a pretty copper for the fox fur, and the tail tips are often crafted into silver daggers popular among young werewolf hunters.

monsterFluff.name: Mariam Shelley
entries: Coldmore villagers, like the nearby woods, are typically quiet and reserved, but innkeeper Mariam Shelley stands apart with her radiant personality. She works her tavern like a well-oiled machine; bold of spirit and full of boundless energy, she floats from table to table like light skipping over a pond. However, her usual vim and vigour have disappeared since her husband and daughter have gone missing. Now, her brow is wrinkled with worry and concern, and her knuckles are white as she absentmindedly twists the hand towel she uses to wipe down tables.

entries.name: Mariam Shelley
entries: {@i Humanoid (halfling), landlady, she/her}

items: {@b Personality.} Bold, energetic, currently worried about family.
{@b Appearance.} Rosy cheeks, thick blond curls, strong forearms. Brow wrinkled with obvious concern.
{@b Desire.} To retrieve family and continue being a source of joy in the dour village of Coldmore.

entries: What'll it be? Grandma's Old Peculiar? Liverwort ale? How about nightshade for those bloody beasts out there.

monsterFluff.name: Franklin Shelley
entries: A seasoned hunter and scout whose hunting party was savagely attacked by the Bloodhound. As the sole survivor, he was deliberately allowed to escape so that word of the beast's menace might spread and lure adventurers into the forest. Gravely wounded, Franklin collapsed before reaching home, his body bearing the marks of the brutal encounter.

monsterFluff.name: Claire Shelley
entries: Franklin's fearless and precocious daughter, tracked her father down after sensing something was wrong. Resourceful beyond her years, she built a campfire and has been tending to his wounds with determination and care.

monsterFluff.name: Jandar Olana
entries: The cheerful young man that Jandar Olana has grown into is far removed from the grim orphan Archbishop Cyril rescued from the streets to mentor and raise in the Empyreal Solar. Slender, with curly red hair and a dash of freckles across his pale cheeks, Jandar's unwavering optimism and energy are a testament to his faith in the Empyreal Solar. For the past two years, the enthusiastic missionary has travelled the region distributing food, supplies, and hope to the poor and needy. He was recalled early from his latest mission and instructed to gather supplies before returning home for an upcoming feast.

entries.name: Jandar Olana
entries: {@i Humanoid (human), priest, he/him}

items: {@b Personality.} Fidgety and highly energetic, head in the clouds so often he forgets to eat.
{@b Appearance.} Lanky, red hair, pale-skinned, simple red and white priest's vestments.
{@b Desire.} To help those around him in whatever way he can and make his mentor proud.
{@b Fear.} Losing the family that is his church and congregation.

entries: Are you okay? Is there anything you need? How can I be of service?

monsterFluff.name: Mary Oswalt
entries: Mary Oswalt is the incredibly friendly and helpful Mayor of Cresthill. Well into her fifties, Mary uses her kind demeanour to hide a shrewd and calculating mind. However, like the other townsfolk, she has blind faith in the clergy and is eager to attend the special services. Mary has developed a heavy cough after being accidentally exposed to some of the Pontiff's smoke during today's service. Her eyes are watery, and she is paler than usual. She puts on a brave face for the townsfolk, even though she is clearly struggling.

monsterFluff.name: Lorenzo Pastuli
entries: Lorenzo Pastuli has been Cresthill's organ player for over three decades. Lorenzo was born blind, and music is his life. He was devastated when the clergy told him he was not allowed to play. The priests said music was a distraction and silent contemplation was the way to enlightenment. The bard is now attempting to give a performance, which is causing controversy among the townsfolk.

monsterFluff.name: Sir Laris the Penitent
entries: After the Rose Knight's flesh transformed into an oozing monstrosity, their soul fractured and a small, desperate spark survived. That spark clung to the former knight's bones, which crawled out of the swamp. Disguised in patchwork plate armour and calling themself Sir Laris the Penitent, the now-undead warrior explains that he is the sole survivor of the Platinum Fist and that he wants to recover the remains of his fallen comrades after defeating the dark creature living in the bog. He doesn't reveal his undead nature, nor disclose that the creature was born from his desperate actions.

Sir Laris can be convinced to join the characters as a sidekick with a successful {@footnote VDC|13/15/17} Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check. He uses the statistics of a {@creature knight} for the 5th-level hunt, or a {@creature gladiator} wearing plate armour (AC 18) for the 10th- and 15th-level hunts, but is undead and is immune to poison. Otherwise, he secretly follows them into the swamp at a distance. If his undead nature is discovered, he admits the truth that he was once a knight of the Platinum Fist before the monster ended his life. A successful VDC Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check reveals that Sir Laris isn't being completely truthful, but is sincere in wanting to defeat the monster and recover the knights' remains.

entries.name: Sir Laris
entries: {@i Undead, ex-knight, he/him*}

items: {@b Personality.} Somber and haunted, Rose Knight's skeleton in disguise.
{@b Appearance.} A skeleton hidden beneath voluminous dark robes, a thin lace veil hides his face.
{@b Desire.} To defeat the ooze knight and wrest his soul back from its gooey prison.
{@b Fear.} That the Platinum Fist's sacrifice will be in vein.

entries: Do any of you possess the mettle to rid Dullowmarch of this nightmare?
*While the Rose Knight identifies as non-gendered, their disguise, Sir Laris, is male.

monsterFluff.name: Sir Erik the Gallant
entries: Sir Erik the Gallant was the only survivor of a band of adventurers who met their doom battling the "black knight". During the battle, the knight flooded Erik's mind with a psychic assault, causing him to attack his erstwhile allies in his madness. He has since developed acute dragopathy, a disease caused by exposure to a chromatic dragon's will where one oscillates between their various, pernicious emotions, moment to moment. A successful {@footnote VDC|13/15/17} Intelligence or Wisdom ({@skill Arcana} or {@skill Medicine}) check identifies his behaviour as the symptoms of dragopathy.

monsterFluff.name: Priestess Gameela Haddad
entries: The Shai'tan Queen, Lysta the Wise, was horrified to discover the monster that her beloved Celarian had become and fled the island under the guise of Gameela Haddad, priestess of the goddess of peace. Unwavering in her duty, she has been searching for any who might help end the demonic siege and stop her husband's cruelty.

entries.name: Gameela Haddad
entries: {@i Humanoid (elf), queen/priestess, she/her}

items: {@b Personality.} Stoic, patient, resolute. Though kind, she is able to make hard choices.
{@b Appearance.} Long silver hair, pale skin, deep emerald eyes. Wears finely cut gold-and-white robes that have seen better days.
{@b Desire.} To save her people and restore Shai'taryn.
{@b Fear.} That desperation will lead her to despicable and blasphemous acts.

entries: As leaders, we do what we must to ensure the safety of our people—but some lines must never be crossed. Journey before destination.

monsterFluff.name: Emissary Aart Oodee Tu
entries: An Emissary of the Curators, Aart Oodee Tu has spent time studying the effects of the corruption on the mountain where CRE's portal meets the Material Plane. With the data they have gathered, they have devised a method to reverse CRE's deterioration and now seek a party of capable adventurers to protect them while they complete the upload inside the quarantined section of the Clockwork Arcadia.

Aart Oodee Tu is an automaton shaped like a trash can made of wood and metal that moves on three wheels. They navigate stairs via a levitation mechanism and have small, mechanical appendages that project from hidden compartments in their body when they need to interact with objects. Aart is fluent in every known language and projects a blue, semi-opaque illusion when speaking to the party. The projection is of a human woman, hair wrapped in tight buns and dressed in a plain robe, that speaks in a calm, measured tone. The Curators believed that this method of communication would make mortals more inclined to work with a construct.

entries.name: Emissary Aart Oodee Tu
entries: {@i Construct, ambassador, they/them}

items: {@b Personality.} Respectful and patient, they enjoy mortal company and do not share the disdain that many other constructs have for organics.
{@b Appearance.} A wood and metal cylinder with a domed top that rolls on three wheels.
{@b Desire.} To spread the delights of the Clockwork Arcadia's perfect order.

entries: So then the excess waste is used to grow new energy sources? Which in turn creates new excess waste? Fascinating.

monsterFluff.name: Unit 83A5T
entries: Unit 83A5T aka Pumecha. A pumecha is an artifice-enhanced mountain lion that was among CRE's first animal-hybrid creations. Its motorised legs allow it to chase down its prey for miles without tiring. With steel claws and fangs able to rip through plate armour, it has no fear of facing hunters or adventurers head-on.

entries.name: Corrupted Constructs
entries: The forge is filled with CRE's corrupted constructs, which are under its complete control. Many of the constructs have been augmented with organic parts or elemental components not typical of a construct.
CRE's creations appear as vaguely humanoid geometric shapes constructed of metal frames, cogs, wheels, and springs.

monsterFluff.name: Nissa Bellyblock
entries: Nissa Bellyblock is the bubbly owner of the Solarium Botanica.
